audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for women supporters, sower, lamp, family redefinition, storm calming, Gerasene release, Jairus, hemorrhaging woman, and daughter's raising.

verses.restored: Soon afterward he traveled through cities and villages, proclaiming and announcing the good news of the reign of the Divine; the twelve were with him,
and certain women who had been healed from harmful spirits and weaknesses: Mary called Magdalene, from whom seven unclean spirits had gone out,
Joanna wife of Chuza, Herod's steward, Susanna, and many others, who were serving them from their resources.
When a great crowd was gathering and people from city after city came to him, he spoke through a parable.
The sower went out to sow his seed. As he sowed, some fell beside the way and was trampled, and the birds of heaven devoured it.
Other seed fell on rock, and growing up it withered because it had no moisture.
Other seed fell in the middle of thorns, and the thorns grew with it and choked it.
Other seed fell into beautiful/good earth, and growing, it made fruit a hundredfold. Saying these things, he called out, Whoever has ears to hear, let that one hear.
His disciples questioned him about what this parable might be.
He said, To you it has been given to know the mysteries of the reign of the Divine, but to the rest in parables, so that seeing they may not see, and hearing they may not understand.
This is the parable: the seed is the word of the Divine.
Those beside the way are the ones who hear; then the adversarial one comes and takes away the word from their heart, so that they may not trust and be saved/healed.
Those on the rock are the ones who, when they hear, receive the word with joy; these have no root, who trust for a season and in time of testing fall away.
What fell into thorns, these are the ones who hear, and as they go are choked by worries, wealth, and pleasures of life, and do not bring fruit to completion.
What fell in beautiful/good earth, these are the ones who, hearing the word in a beautiful/good and noble heart, hold it fast and bear fruit in endurance.
No one lighting a lamp covers it with a vessel or puts it under a bed, but places it on a lampstand, so those entering may see the light.
For nothing hidden will not become visible, nor secret that will not be known and come into the open.
Therefore watch how you hear; for whoever has, more will be given, and whoever does not have, even what one thinks one has will be taken away.
His mother and brothers came to him and were not able to meet him because of the crowd.
It was reported to him, Your mother and your brothers are standing outside wanting to see you.
He answered them, My mother and my brothers are those who hear the word of the Divine and do it.
One day he entered a boat with his disciples and said to them, Let us cross over to the other side of the lake. They set sail.
As they sailed, he fell asleep. A windstorm came down on the lake, and they were filling with water and were in danger.
They came and woke him, saying, Master, Master, we are perishing. He woke and rebuked the wind and the surge of water; they ceased, and there was calm.
He said to them, Where is your trust? Being afraid, they marveled, saying to one another, Who then is this, that he commands even winds and water, and they obey him?
They sailed down to the region of the Gerasenes, which is opposite Galilee.
When he came out onto the land, a man from the city met him who had unclean spirits; for a long time he had worn no garment and did not remain in a house but among tombs.
Seeing Jesus, he cried out, fell before him, and said with a great voice, What to me and to you, Jesus, Son of the Most High Divine? I beg you, do not torment me.

For Jesus had commanded the unclean spirit to come out from the man. Many times it had seized him; he had been guarded, bound with chains and shackles, yet breaking the bonds, he was driven by the troubling force into deserted places.
Jesus asked him, What is your name? He said, Legion, because many unclean spirits had entered him.
They pleaded with him that he would not command them to go away into the abyss.
A considerable herd of pigs was feeding there on the mountain; they pleaded that he would permit them to enter those. He permitted them.
The unclean spirits came out from the man and entered the pigs, and the herd rushed down the steep bank into the lake and was drowned.
Those feeding them, seeing what had happened, fled and reported it in the city and in the fields.
They went out to see what had happened and came to Jesus; they found the man from whom the unclean spirits had gone out, clothed and sound-minded, sitting at Jesus' feet, and they were afraid.
Those who had seen reported to them how the one troubled by unclean spirits was saved/healed.
All the multitude of the surrounding Gerasene region asked him to depart from them, because they were held by great fear. Entering the boat, he returned.
The man from whom the spirits had gone out begged to be with him, but Jesus sent him away, saying,
Return to your house and describe how much the Divine has done for you. He went away, proclaiming through the whole city how much Jesus had done for him.
When Jesus returned, the crowd welcomed him, for they were all waiting for him.
Look, a man named Jairus came, and he was a ruler of the synagogue. Falling at Jesus' feet, he pleaded with him to come to his house,
because he had an only daughter, about twelve years old, and she was dying. As he went, the crowds were pressing around him.
A woman with a flow of blood for twelve years, who had spent all her living on physicians and was not able to be healed by anyone,
came behind and touched the fringe of his garment; immediately the flow of her blood stopped.
Jesus said, Who touched me? When all denied it, Peter said, Master, the crowds surround you and press in.
But Jesus said, Someone touched me, for I knew power had gone out from me.
The woman, seeing that she was not hidden, came trembling; falling before him, she declared before all the people why she had touched him and how she was immediately healed.
He said to her, Daughter, your trust has saved/healed you; go into peace.
While he was still speaking, someone comes from the synagogue ruler's house, saying, Your daughter has died; trouble the teacher no more.
Jesus, hearing, answered him, Do not fear; only trust, and she will be saved/healed.
Coming to the house, he did not allow anyone to enter with him except Peter, John, James, and the child's father and mother.
All were weeping and mourning for her. He said, Do not weep, for she has not died but sleeps.
They laughed at him, knowing that she had died.
Taking her by the hand, he called out, Child, rise.
Her spirit returned, and she rose immediately. He ordered that something be given her to eat.
Her parents were astonished, but he instructed them to tell no one what had happened.
